name: Flipkart DSA Algorithm Suite
scenario: Flipkart's technical screen requires solving 5 array problems in 45 minutes. Each combines 2+ patterns. Build a complete solution suite demonstrating pattern recognition.

core.description: maxSubarray(int[] nums): Kadane's O(n) O(1). Test: [-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4] → 6.
majorityElement(int[] nums): Boyer-Moore O(n) O(1). Test: [3,2,3] → 3, [2,2,1,1,1,2,2] → 2.
nextPermutation(int[] nums): in-place O(n) O(1). Test: [1,2,3] → [1,3,2], [3,2,1] → [1,2,3].
README: Pattern recognition guide — which pattern for which problem type.

bonus.description: rotateArray(int[] nums, int k): in-place O(n) O(1) using 3 reversals. Test: [1,2,3,4,5,6,7] k=3 → [5,6,7,1,2,3,4].
jumpGame(int[] nums): O(n) greedy. Can reach last index? Test: [2,3,1,1,4] → true, [3,2,1,0,4] → false.
findDuplicate(int[] nums): O(n) O(1) Floyd's cycle detection. Test: [1,3,4,2,2] → 2.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Kadane: curr=max(nums[i], curr+nums[i]); restart when prev sum negative. Boyer-Moore: candidate=first elem, count=1; iterate: same→count++, diff→count--; if count==0 reset candidate. Majority exists → candidate is correct. Next permutation: (1) find rightmost i where nums[i]<nums[i+1] (2) find rightmost j where nums[j]>nums[i] (3) swap i,j (4) reverse i+1..end. Longest consecutive: add all to HashSet, for each n: if n-1 not in set (streak start), count streak forward. Rotate: 3 reversals — reverse(0,n-1), reverse(0,k-1), reverse(k,n-1).
